description: General setup for inheritance splits across a securities portfolio with round-lot constraints. Defines the algorithm concepts (target weights, target/rounded shares, per-position deviation, running cumulative deviation), the three beneficiary portfolios (mother + child one + child two), and the formulas that drive the splitting walk. Companion files: portfolio_instruments.json (security catalogue + currency assignment), portfolio_report.json (bank-reported holdings, prices, FX rates), portfolio_split_parameters_sample.json (concrete 30/35/35 split agreement and sample per-position allocations). The algorithm walks positions in decreasing order of (share price * round lot), picks the rounded share count closest to child 1's residual target, mirrors that to child 2, leaves the rest to the mother, and rolls each rounding deviation into the next position so cumulative drift stays bounded.

words.name: portfolio
words.description: a grouping of financial assets such as stocks, bonds, commodities, currencies and cash equivalents held by an investor

words.name: original
words.description: the starting state of the portfolio before the inheritance split is performed

words.name: target
words.description: an intended end state, here the value or share count a beneficiary should end up holding

words.name: one
words.description: the cardinal number 1, used here as the identifier of the first child's portfolio

words.name: two
words.description: the cardinal number 2, used here as the identifier of the second child's portfolio

words.name: mother
words.description: female parent; here the original owner of the portfolio who retains 30%

words.name: child
words.description: offspring; here each of the two beneficiaries who receive 35% of the portfolio

words.name: share
words.description: unit of ownership of a corporation; one of the equal parts into which a company's capital is divided

words.name: shares
words.description: plural of share; the total count of equity units of a security held in a position

words.name: price
words.description: the amount of money expected, required, or given in payment for something; for bonds typically quoted as a percentage of nominal value

words.name: round
words.description: qualifier indicating an integer multiple (e.g. of a lot size)

words.name: lot
words.description: in trading, a standard tradable unit size of a security

words.name: nominal
words.description: describing the face value of a security as opposed to its market value

words.name: value
words.description: the amount of money for which something can be exchanged in the market; in this file always denominated in CHF unless otherwise qualified

words.name: position
words.description: the amount of a particular security held by an investor

words.name: total
words.description: qualifier denoting the sum across all elements of a set

words.name: weight
words.description: the relative share of a portfolio that a position or beneficiary accounts for

words.name: percent
words.description: a number or ratio expressed as a fraction of 100

words.name: deviation
words.description: in this file, the signed difference between the rounded allocated value and the unrounded target value for a beneficiary at a single position

words.name: cumulative
words.description: qualifier denoting a running sum across positions processed so far in the splitting walk

triples.name: round lot
triples.description: the minimum tradable quantity of a security (in shares for equities or in nominal currency units for bonds)

triples.name: share price
triples.description: the per-share market price of an equity

triples.name: nominal value
triples.description: the face value of a bond holding (the amount on which the coupon is computed)

triples.name: position value
triples.description: the current market value of a single holding (shares * share price, or nominal * price percentage for bonds)

triples.name: portfolio value
triples.description: the sum of the market values of all positions in a portfolio, expressed in the portfolio reference currency (CHF here)

triples.name: target weight
triples.description: the desired share of the portfolio value that a beneficiary should receive (mother 0.30, each child 0.35)

triples.name: target value
triples.description: the absolute CHF amount a beneficiary should receive (portfolio value * target weight)

triples.name: target shares
triples.description: the unrounded number of shares of a given position that would exactly match a beneficiary's residual target value at that position's share price

triples.name: rounded shares
triples.description: the number of shares actually allocated, equal to round(target shares / round lot) * round lot

triples.name: cumulative deviation
triples.description: for a given beneficiary and position, the running sum (over positions already split) of (allocated value - target value); subtracted from the next position's target value to keep drift bounded

triples.name: mother portfolio
triples.description: the parent's share of the original portfolio (target weight 0.30)

formulas.name: stock position value
formulas.description: market value of an equity position in its trading currency: number of shares held times current share price

formulas.name: bond position value
formulas.description: market value of a bond holding: nominal value times current price expressed as a percentage of par (e.g. 99.5%)

formulas.name: target value per beneficiary
formulas.description: the CHF amount a beneficiary should receive in total, given the agreed split weights

formulas.name: unrounded target shares
formulas.description: how many shares of a given position would exactly match the residual target value for one beneficiary at the current share price; the residual subtracts cumulative deviation from previous, larger-round-lot positions

formulas.name: rounded share allocation
formulas.description: actual number of shares allocated to a beneficiary, snapped to the nearest multiple of the security's round lot

formulas.name: mirror second child
formulas.description: child 2 receives the same rounded share count as child 1 to keep the two children's portfolios identical at every step; their drift accumulates symmetrically

formulas.name: mother gets the remainder
formulas.description: the mother's allocation is whatever is left after both children have been served, so the original total in each position is conserved

formulas.name: position-level deviation
formulas.description: the per-position contribution to the running deviation for a child: how much the rounded allocation overshoots or undershoots the per-position target value

formulas.name: cumulative deviation roll-forward
formulas.description: the running deviation carried into the next, smaller-round-lot position; the algorithm walks positions in decreasing order of (share price * round lot) so later positions can compensate earlier rounding error

formulas.name: beneficiary portfolio total
formulas.description: the realised total value each beneficiary ends up with; the sum of the position values across all of that beneficiary's allocations
